summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a classic ’83 show with scorching Scarlet>Fire, a full‑band Spanish Jam and a feverish Other One.
review: The SBD source is remarkably clear – listeners repeatedly note the “crisp”, “superb” and “clean” mix despite an occasional Althea skip. Musically the night shines on Scarlet>Fire, the extended Spanish Jam, The Other One and a punchy Bertha‑>Promised Land opener; even the usual ’83 quirks feel fresh, though Althea drags a bit. Overall fans deem it one of the stronger 1983 performances.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (≈70%) are 4‑5 stars and praise both the performance and the crystal‑clear soundboard, outweighing a few isolated complaints about Althea and minor speed quirks.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Exceptionally clear SBD with strong instrument separation; one reviewer notes a brief skip in Althea and occasional perception of speed variation, but overall the mix is crisp, balanced and free of crowd noise.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set opens with a high‑energy Bertha→Promised Land, rolls through classic ’83 staples, hits a monster Scarlet>Fire segue into Fire, and caps the second set with a soaring Spanish Jam and Wharf Rat jam; pacing is praised despite a slightly shorter second half.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar tone on Scarlet>Fire and Promised Land is repeatedly called “on fire” and “perfect”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for a tight intro on The Other One.
band_member_comments.Bob: Celebrated for enthusiastic vocals, laughing during Wharf Rat, and occasional slide work (sometimes “not contained”).
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards receive specific praise (“nice in the mix”, “great Spanish Jam keys”), though one mix is said to have a bit too much Brent.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey’s interplay is highlighted as varied and interesting; drums and Space are described as “varied and interesting” and even audible when PA failed.
